title: The Universe's Baby Picture
script_summary: Explore the cosmic microwave background, the universe's faint glow from its infancy.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you the universe has a baby picture?
It’s called the cosmic microwave background, or CMB.
This light is leftover energy from when the universe became transparent.
Imagine, just 380,000 years after the Big Bang, it was released into space.
And now, it's all around us, a faint glow we can detect.
This cosmic snapshot helps us understand the universe’s infancy and evolution.
Want to learn more cosmic secrets? Hit that follow button!
